_meta.note: Built to the Armagh-locked standard. Leitrim, a Division 4 side, produced the shock of the Connacht series by beating higher-division Sligo away, reached the Connacht semi-final, then played a three-game Tailteann Cup campaign. Five championship games in all.
_meta.verification: All scorelines, venues and referees verified against primary match reports (gaa.ie, RTE, Irish Times, Irish News, Gaelic Life, Down News) and the official Connacht GAA and 2026 Tailteann Cup brackets. Scorer detail sourced where published; honest nulls elsewhere.

manager.name: Stephen Poacher
manager.note: A Down native managing Leitrim. Took a Division 4 side to a shock Connacht quarter-final win over Sligo and a competitive provincial semi-final against Galway. Faced his home county Down in the Tailteann Cup Round 1.

source.snippet: Down native Stephen Poacher meets a familiar foe, with his Leitrim side hosting Carlow in round 2B.

league.note: Finished second from bottom of Division 4, the lowest tier, which is what made the Connacht win over Division 3 Sligo a genuine shock. Struggled badly through the league campaign.

source.snippet: Leitrim finished second from bottom in Division Four and the Yeatsmen, who operated in Division Three of the National League, were favoured to see them off in this championship opener.

Up in Carrick-on-Shannon, Wexford did all their business in the first half by stretching 0-14 to no score clear of Leitrim... Leitrim half-time substitute Jack Kelly ended with 1-3, including a late goal from a close-range free to trim the final margin to five - 0-20 to 1-12.

scorers.note: 1 two-pointer, 1 free; 16th-minute goal into empty net

source.snippet: Brilliant Leitrim beat Sligo for the first time in the Connacht Senior Football Championship since 2011 in defiantly squeezing over the line by a one-point margin... Barry McNulty, Keith Keegan and captain Ryan O'Rourke each netted.

results.note: The shock of the Connacht series: a Division 4 side beating Division 3 Sligo away for their first championship win over them since 2011. Three goals (McNulty, Keegan, O'Rourke) built a 10-point lead before holding on by one as Sligo rallied. Partial scorer list (the three goals plus a McNulty two-pointer are confirmed; full point breakdown not fully itemised). Sligo's two-pointers (3) read from the same report. Referee: Fergal Kelly.

Lost the Connacht semi-final to Galway by five points but gave a competitive account; Tom Prior was named man of the match in this game per later coverage. Full scorer list not yet sourced; Galway's file carries the opposing perspective.

A 14-point defeat away to a promoted Division 2 Down side. Leitrim played without key men Barry McNulty (broke his foot in the Galway game) and Tom Prior, both injured. Goalkeeper Killian Gaffey top-scored with 0-5 including two two-point frees. This is a Round 1 loss, sending Leitrim into the Round 2B knockout bracket. Down's two-pointers (4) read from the same report. Referee: T Murphy (Galway).

results.venue: Heartland Credit Union Páirc Seán MacDiarmada, Carrick-on-Shannon

scorers.note: 3 two-pointers; 44th-min two-pointer put Leitrim ahead for the first time

source.snippet: Tailteann Cup - Round 2B Leitrim 0-22 1-13 Carlow Venue: Heartland Credit Union Páirc Seán MacDiarmada

results.note: Last-chance R2B win to stay in the competition, coming good in the second half with Paul Honeyman leading the scoring. Full scorer list not yet sourced. Leitrim were seeking back-to-back Tailteann wins on home soil.

results.venue: Heartland Credit Union Páirc Seán MacDiarmada, Carrick-on-Shannon

scorers.note: 3 two-pointers, 1 free; sparked the second-half revival
scorers.source_note: Kevin Egan match report, Páirc Seán Mac Diarmada

scorers.note: substitute; rifled a 20m free to the net late on

scorers.note: substitute; first Leitrim score, opening the second half

source.snippet: Wexford did all their business in the first half by stretching 0-14 to no score clear of Leitrim... Oisín McLoughlin kicked three quick-fire two-pointers to bring Leitrim inside double digits... Jack Kelly ended with 1-3, including a late goal.

results.note: Elimination game. Leitrim were 0-14 to no score down at one stage before Oisin McLoughlin's three two-pointers and a late Jack Kelly goal trimmed the final margin to five. Both sides finished with 14 men (Conor Quinn sent off for Leitrim). Partial scorer list (the two standout contributors confirmed; full breakdown not itemised). Wexford's two-pointers (2: Nolan, Brooks) read from the same report.

Complete point-by-point breakdown for the Sligo and Wexford games (standout scorers confirmed, full lists not itemised)
collection_meta.notes: Leitrim's headline is the Sligo shock, the standout upset of the Connacht series and a clear candidate for a signature panel or a season-narrative beat (Division 4 beats Division 3 away, three goals, holds on by one). Barry McNulty (man of the match v Sligo, broke his foot v Galway) and Tom Prior (man of the match v Galway) are the names, though both missed the Tailteann run injured. A complete five-game record despite the lighter scorer detail in the lower-profile games.
